actionables.other_change: Respondents must prepare for and attend regulatory hearings
actionables.status_change: Inability to rely on crowdfunding exemption due to payment to principal
actionables.process_change: Ensure strict adherence to prospectus and exemption filing requirements
actionables.reporting_change: File accurate and compliant reports of exempt distribution

other_dates.description: Relevant Period when 2307755 raised funds
Incorporation date of 2307755 Alberta Ltd.
Date when payment of $21,000 was made to Astete

impact_summary.objective: To notify the respondent of a hearing regarding alleged illegal securities distributions and breaches of the Securities Act.
impact_summary.risk_impact: Non-compliance risks include enforcement actions, potential orders against principals and issuers, and reputational damage.
impact_summary.what_changed: The Alberta Securities Commission is initiating enforcement proceedings against 2307755 Alberta Ltd. and its principal for distributing securities without a prospectus or valid exemption.
impact_summary.why_it_matters: This update highlights regulatory enforcement on crowdfunding exemptions and prospectus requirements, emphasizing compliance risks for issuers and principals in securities offerings.
key_requirements: Issuers must comply with prospectus requirements or valid exemptions when distributing securities.
No commission or similar payments to principals or employees are allowed under crowdfunding exemptions.
Respondents must attend hearings or risk orders being made in their absence.

rules: Commission Rule 15-501 – Rules of Practice and Procedure for Commission Proceedings
National Instrument 45-110 – Start-Up Crowdfunding Registration and Prospectus Exemptions
personnel: Samir Sabharwal, Executive Director

penalties_consequences: Potential orders under sections 198, 199 and 202 of the Securities Act
Enforcement actions may proceed in absence of respondent
Reputational and financial consequences from illegal securities distribution

metadata.title: Notice Of Hearing
metadata.summary: Notice of hearing regarding alleged securities violations by Daniel Rodolfo Astete and 2307755 Alberta Ltd.

jurisdiction_tier.label: international
jurisdiction_tier.reasoning: The Alberta Securities Commission is a provincial regulator in Canada, which is a non-US country-level regulator.

entities: Financial and Consumer Services Commission (New Brunswick)

actionables.other_change: Implement transition provisions for benchmarks designated before the effective date.
actionables.policy_change: Update internal policies to align with revised assurance report requirements and timing.
actionables.process_change: Adjust processes to ensure assurance reports are prepared and submitted within specified timelines and applicable periods.
actionables.training_change: Train relevant staff on new assurance report requirements and timelines.
actionables.reporting_change: Modify reporting schedules to comply with new fixed dates and 90-day submission deadlines for assurance reports.
actionables.tech_data_change: Update systems to track designation dates, applicable periods, and reporting deadlines for assurance reports.

other_dates.description: Publication date of CSA Notice and Request for Comment on proposed amendments
Final publication date of CDOR benchmark
Date of OSC revocation order for CDOR and RBSL

impact_summary.objective: To amend MI 25-102 and Companion Policy 25-102 to revise assurance report requirements for designated benchmarks and their administrators, addressing technical issues and providing greater certainty.
impact_summary.risk_impact: Reduces uncertainty and potential compliance risk related to assurance reporting; mitigates operational and reputational risks for benchmark administrators and contributors.
impact_summary.what_changed: Revised assurance report requirements for designated critical, interest rate, commodity, and other benchmarks; introduced new section 13.1 for other designated benchmarks; simplified language on timing and applicable periods for assurance reports; added transition provisions for pre-existing designations.
impact_summary.why_it_matters: Ensures clarity and consistency in assurance reporting obligations, addressing prior technical issues encountered by accounting firms, thereby reducing regulatory risk and enhancing oversight effectiveness.
key_requirements: First reasonable assurance report to be provided on a fixed date after benchmark designation.
Applicable period for each assurance report specified, with first report having a 3-month look-back period.
Assurance reports to be provided no later than 90 days after the end of the applicable period.
References to code of conduct for benchmark contributors included in assurance report requirements.
Transition provisions for interest rate benchmarks designated before effective date without benchmark contributors.

rules: Multilateral Instrument 25-102 Designated Benchmarks and Benchmark Administrators (MI 25-102) (https://www.osc.ca/en/securities-law/instruments-rules-policies/25-102-designated-benchmarks-and-benchmark-administrators)
other_ref: OSC revocation order for CDOR and RBSL (https://www.osc.ca/sites/default/files/2024-07/ord_20240718_refinitiv-benchmark-services.pdf)
AMF revocation order for CDOR and RBSL (https://lautorite.qc.ca/fileadmin/lautorite/professionnels/structures-marche/indice-reference/2024-PDG-0044.pdf)
Memorandum of Understanding respecting oversight of designated benchmarks (https://www.osc.ca/sites/default/files/2021-05/mou_20210527_designated-benchmarks.pdf)
personnel: Michael Bennett, Senior Legal Counsel, Corporate Finance, Ontario Securities Commission
Darren Sutherland, Senior Accountant, Corporate Finance, Ontario Securities Commission
Serge Boisvert, Senior Policy Coordinator, Autorité des marchés financiers
Roland Geiling, Derivatives Product Analyst, Autorité des marchés financiers
Marie-Andrée Beaulieu, Senior Policy Advisor, Autorité des marchés financiers
Harvey Steblyk, Senior Legal Counsel, Market Regulation, Alberta Securities Commission
Janice Cherniak, Senior Legal Counsel, Market Regulation, Alberta Securities Commission
Michael Brady, Deputy Director, Capital Markets Regulation, British Columbia Securities Commission
Faisal Kirmani, Senior Analyst, Derivatives, British Columbia Securities Commission
past_release: CSA Notice and Request for Comment on Proposed Amendments to MI 25-102 and Companion Policy 25-102 dated May 30, 2024 (https://www.osc.ca/sites/default/files/2024-05/57CF8315CC3A43DF9004451E86F98398.ashx)

other_notes: Includes designated benchmark administrators and benchmark contributors
Applies to designated critical benchmarks, interest rate benchmarks, commodity benchmarks, and other designated benchmarks

penalties_consequences: Non-compliance may result in regulatory sanctions including fines and reputational damage.
Failure to provide timely and accurate assurance reports could lead to enforcement actions by securities regulators.

metadata.title: CSA Notice of Amendments to Multilateral Instrument 25-102 Designated Benchmarks and Benchmark Administrators and Changes to Companion Policy 25-102 Designated Benchmarks and Benchmark Administrators
